steps.title: Identify the opening em-dash
steps.content: The em-dash after Mirounga angustirostris introduces an alternative (“—or northern elephant seals, as they're more commonly known). This opening em-dash begins a construction that must close before the main clause begins.

steps.title: Close with a matching em-dash
steps.content:
When an em-dash opens a construction mid-sentence, a matching em-dash must close it before the main clause.

Choice B provides this: known—a 2021 study. Choices A and D use commas instead, breaking punctuation consistency.

Choice C breaks the sentence inappropriately. That matches choice B — B is correct.
